narrative: OMX30 opens to a split global backdrop: Continental Europe rallying hard (+1.49% DAX, +0.44% CAC) while US and Asia retreat, creating a neutral setup for Stockholm. The index remains in bull regime 6.6% above its 200-MA and 90-day momentum is strong (+5.3% ROC), but technical internals are mixed—MACD below signal, RSI at 44 (neutral), and ADX 21.6 with bearish DI bias limiting conviction. Oil's +1.89% climb and weak EUR/SEK (-0.17%) create cross-currents: industrials face input cost headwinds while exporters lack currency support. The 52-week position at 79% of range suggests limited room for fresh upside without a catalyst.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens flat to slightly positive, following European lead but capped by divergent US signals (S&P -0.45%, NASDAQ -0.90%) and oil strength pressuring input costs. Key technical: watch 3085 (lower Bollinger) as support; resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) face headwinds from Brent +1.89% but may find safety in relative European outperformance. Banks neutral—US 10Y +9bp is modest NIM benefit (+0.09%) but offset by mixed risk sentiment. Boliden a bright spot on copper +0.94%, gold stable; Ericsson/Hexagon follow NASDAQ weakness. H&M pressure from EUR/SEK weakness (sourcing cost drag). ADX 21.6 with bearish DI keeps position-sizing light; watch VIX at 18.58 (creeping up +0.81%) for risk-off acceleration. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is intraday trigger.
drivers: European strength (DAX +1.49%, FTSE +1.26%) vs US/Asia weakness creating mixed signal
Oil +1.89% pressures industrials (28% of index) despite European resilience
OMX30 bull regime intact (+6.6% vs 200-MA) but RSI 44 and MACD below signal flag consolidation risk
USD/SEK flat (-0.08%) reduces export tailwind; EUR/SEK weakness (-0.17%) pressures H&M sourcing
ADX 21.6 moderate with bearish DI bias—confidence capped at Medium per rules

sectorImpacts.reason: Brent oil +1.89% to 111.3 increases input costs; partially offset by DAX strength but ADX 21.6 (moderate, bearish DI) caps confidence.
US 10Y yield +0.09% to 4.599% supports NIM outlook, but small move and mixed risk sentiment limit upside.
Copper +0.94% and gold stable provide direct revenue lift; strong commodity backdrop.
NASDAQ -0.90% overnight pressures sentiment; NASDAQ correlation r=0.49 provides downside drag.
EUR/SEK -0.17% weakens sourcing cost base; oil inflation pressures discretionary spending.
US 10Y +0.09% is marginal refinancing pressure; not yet material stress signal.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.94% and gold holding firm provide direct revenue tailwind; strong commodities backdrop.
Oil +1.89% pressures input costs; industrials headwind; USD/SEK flat removes export boost.
NASDAQ -0.90% overnight creates negative sentiment carry; tech correlation r=0.49.
EUR/SEK weakness (-0.17%) increases sourcing costs; oil inflation pressures discretionary.
Modest NIM benefit from +0.09% in yields; mixed risk sentiment offsets; monitor for deepening weakness.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (working assumption for yesterday close based on +0.5% vs 50-MA)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish data releases scheduled. US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is primary intraday catalyst; expect volatility around print.
topRisk: VIX creeping higher (+0.81% to 18.58) signals risk-off undertone; if US data disappoints or trade headlines escalate, VIX spike could trigger broad OMX30 sell-off. ADX 21.6 with bearish DI already suggests directional weakness—position sizing should remain light. Overnight NASDAQ -0.90% may roll into Stockholm open.
technicalSummary: MACD below signal (hist=-7.841) flags momentum divergence
ADX 21.6 moderate with bearish DI bias—reduce confidence per rules
Bollinger %B=0.31 in lower half, suggesting room to mean reversion
ROC-90 +5.3% strong but 52W at 79% range leaves limited upside without catalyst
Stochastic K=30.0 D=24.9 in neutral zone—no directional urgency
